title: Gentianella buchtienii
taxonomy: Gentianella buchtienii is classified in the family Gentianaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gentian family. Gentianella buchtienii is further classified in the order Gentianales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The order Gentianales contains many species of plants that are known for their colorful flowers and unique growth habits. Within the order Gentianales, Gentianella buchtienii is also classified in the genus Gentianella.

intro: Gentianella buchtienii, also known as Buchtien's gentianella, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae) that is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and fever, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to stimulate appetite, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Gentianella buchtienii include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicrin and swertiamarin, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gentianella buchtienii is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or tea, and is commonly used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, including indigestion and diarrhea.
